params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Student's Warmup data: Red=40, Blue=30, Green=50, Yellow=20. Axis labeled 0-60 in 10s. Red bar highlighted with axis line at 40 glowing.

steps.text: You saw this bar graph in Warmup. Now let's learn how to read it exactly.
See these numbers along the side? They show the scale. 10, 20, 30, 40. That's counting by 10s!
Red has 40. The height of the bar ends at the 40 line.
I can count by 10s to check: 10, 20, 30, 40. You already know how to count by 10s!

elements.description: Vertical bar graph in reading mode. Red=40, Blue=30, Green=50, Yellow=20. Red bar highlighted with 40 axis line glowing. Scale of 10, axis 0-60.

params.description: Blue bar highlighted, height at 30 axis line.

steps.text: Look at Blue. The bar's height ends at a line on the axis. What value does this show?
What value does this show?
30. The bar ends at the 30 line. Same as 3 symbols on the picture graph—each symbol shows 10.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Blue bar highlighted at height 30. Scale of 10, axis 0-60.
Vertical bar graph. Blue bar highlighted, height at 30. Multiple choice tool active.

params.description: Green bar highlighted at height 50.
Axis lines glow sequentially: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50.

steps.text: Green shows 50. Let's count by 10s to check: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50.
You already know how to count by 10s. That's exactly what we do with scale of 10.

params.description: Yellow bar highlighted at height 20.

steps.text: The fourth bar shows Yellow. Count by 10s. What value does the bar show?
What value does the bar show?
20. Two jumps of 10: 10, 20.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Yellow bar highlighted at height 20. Scale of 10, axis 0-60.
Vertical bar graph. Yellow bar highlighted at height 20. Multiple choice tool active.

params.description: Data table appears. Favorite Recess Activities: Swings=30, Tag=50, Slides=40, Jump Rope=20.
Vertical bar graph in creating mode. Scale of 10, axis 0-60. Tag=50, Slides=40, Jump Rope=20 pre-filled. Swings bar empty. Ghost gridlines at 10s visible.

steps.text: Now you create a bar. Swings has 30 votes. Set the height for Swings to show 30.
Set the height for Swings to show 30.

validator.description: Student set Swings bar to 30, correct

params.description: Swings bar now at height 30.

steps.text: 10, 20, 30. The bar ends at 30 on the axis. 30 votes for Swings.

elements.description: Data table showing Favorite Recess Activities values.
Vertical bar graph. All bars filled: Swings=30, Tag=50, Slides=40, Jump Rope=20. Scale of 10, axis 0-60.
Data table showing Favorite Recess Activities values.
Vertical bar graph in creating mode. Swings bar empty, awaiting student input. Tag=50, Slides=40, Jump Rope=20 pre-filled.

params.description: Data table appears. Books Read This Month: Fiction=40, Nonfiction=60, Comics=20, Poetry=30.
Vertical bar graph in creating mode. Scale of 10, axis 0-70. Fiction=40, Comics=20, Poetry=30 pre-filled. Nonfiction bar empty. Ghost gridlines at 10s visible.

steps.text: Nonfiction has 60 books. That's the biggest number. Set the bar for Nonfiction.
Set the bar for Nonfiction to show 60.

validator.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to 60, correct

params.description: Nonfiction bar now at height 60.

steps.text: Nonfiction at 60. You found the 60 line and set the bar there.

elements.description: Data table showing Books Read This Month values.
Vertical bar graph. All bars filled: Fiction=40, Nonfiction=60, Comics=20, Poetry=30. Scale of 10, axis 0-70.
Data table showing Books Read This Month values.
Vertical bar graph in creating mode. Nonfiction bar empty, awaiting student input. Fiction=40, Comics=20, Poetry=30 pre-filled.

params.description: Data table hides from view.
Nonfiction and Comics bars highlighted.

steps.text: Here's the completed graph. Use it to answer a question. How many more books were Nonfiction than Comics?
How many more books were Nonfiction than Comics?
40 more. Nonfiction is 60, Comics is 20. The difference is 40.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Nonfiction and Comics bars highlighted. Fiction=40, Nonfiction=60, Comics=20, Poetry=30. Scale of 10, axis 0-70.
Vertical bar graph. Nonfiction and Comics bars highlighted. All bars complete. Multiple choice tool active.

steps.text: You can read bars that end right at the lines. But what if a bar ends between the lines?

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Stickers Collected: Emma=30 (at 30 line), Jake=25 (between 20-30 lines), Mia=40 (at 40 line), Leo=35 (between 30-40 lines). Scale of 10, axis 0-50. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible. Jake's bar highlighted.

steps.text: Look at Jake's bar. It doesn't end at a line. It ends between the lines.
Remember half-symbols from scale of 2? Half of 2 was 1. Same idea here. Half of 10 is 5.
Half of 10 is 5. So between 20 and 30, that's 20 plus 5, which equals 25. Jake has 25 stickers.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph in reading mode. Stickers Collected. Emma=30, Jake=25, Mia=40, Leo=35. Jake's bar highlighted, ending between 20 and 30 lines. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

params.description: Leo's bar highlighted, ending between 30 and 40 lines.

steps.text: Now look at Leo's bar. It's also between the lines. Leo's bar is between 30 and 40. What value does this show?
What value does Leo's bar show?
35. It's halfway between 30 and 40. Half of 10 is 5, so 30 plus 5 is 35.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Leo's bar highlighted at height 35, between 30 and 40 lines. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.
Vertical bar graph. Leo's bar highlighted, between 30-40 lines. Multiple choice tool active.

params.description: Data table appears. Points Scored: Team A=45, Team B=30, Team C=25, Team D=50, Team E=15.
Vertical bar graph in creating mode. Scale of 10, axis 0-60. Team B=30, Team C=25, Team D=50, Team E=15 pre-filled. Team A bar empty. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

steps.text: Team A scored 45 points. Set the bar for Team A at 45. That's halfway between 40 and 50.
Set the bar for Team A.

validator.description: Student set Team A bar to 45, correct

params.description: Team A bar now at height 45, halfway between 40 and 50.

steps.text: 45. Right between the lines. Halfway from 40 to 50.

elements.description: Data table showing Points Scored values.
Vertical bar graph. All bars filled: Team A=45, Team B=30, Team C=25, Team D=50, Team E=15. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.
Data table showing Points Scored values.
Vertical bar graph in creating mode. Team A bar empty, awaiting student input. Team B=30, Team C=25, Team D=50, Team E=15 pre-filled.

Vertical bar graph in creating mode. Scale of 10, axis 0-60. Runner A=20, Runner B=50, Runner D=45 pre-filled. Runner C bar empty. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

steps.text: Runner C completed 35 laps. Set the bar for Runner C to show 35.
Set the bar for Runner C.

validator.description: Student set Runner C bar to 35, correct

params.description: Runner C bar now at height 35, between 30 and 40.

steps.text: 35. Between 30 and 40. Right in the middle.

params.description: Data table appears. Minutes of Reading: Monday=40, Tuesday=35, Wednesday=50, Thursday=25.
Vertical bar graph in creating mode. Scale of 10, axis 0-60. All bars empty. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

steps.text: Some values are at the axis lines. Some are between. You decide where each bar goes. Try to complete the graph.
Complete the graph.

validator.description: Student set all four bars correctly

params.description: All bars now filled: Monday=40, Tuesday=35, Wednesday=50, Thursday=25.

steps.text: Monday and Wednesday are at the axis lines. Tuesday and Thursday are between the lines. You figured out which is which.

elements.description: Data table showing Minutes of Reading values.
Vertical bar graph. All bars filled: Monday=40 (at line), Tuesday=35 (between), Wednesday=50 (at line), Thursday=25 (between). Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.
Data table showing Minutes of Reading values.
Vertical bar graph in creating mode. All bars empty, awaiting student input. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

params.description: Data table hides from view.
Monday and Tuesday bars highlighted.

steps.text: Use this graph to answer. How many total minutes of reading for Monday and Tuesday?
How many total minutes of reading for Monday and Tuesday?
75 minutes total. Monday is 40, Tuesday is 35. 40 plus 35 is 75.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Monday and Tuesday bars highlighted. Monday=40, Tuesday=35, Wednesday=50, Thursday=25.
Vertical bar graph. Monday and Tuesday bars highlighted. Multiple choice tool active.

steps.text: You've made vertical bar graphs. Now let's try one that goes sideways. Horizontal.

params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears. Votes for Field Trip: Zoo=45, Museum=30, Park=55, Aquarium=40, Theater=35. All 5 bars complete. Scale of 10, axis along bottom 0-60. Categories listed vertically on left. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

steps.text: This bar graph is horizontal. The bars go sideways, but you read them the same way. Finding where the bar ends on the scale.

params.description: Guide points to Zoo bar extending to 45.

steps.text: Zoo's bar extends to 45. Between 40 and 50. Halfway. What value does the Museum bar show?

params.description: Museum bar highlighted.

steps.text: What value does the Museum bar show?
30. The bar ends right at the 30 line.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Museum bar highlighted at value 30. Zoo=45, Museum=30, Park=55, Aquarium=40, Theater=35.
Horizontal bar graph. Museum bar highlighted. Multiple choice tool active.

params.description: Park bar highlighted, extending to 55 (between 50-60).

steps.text: Look at the Park bar. What value does the Park bar represent?
What value does the Park bar represent?
55. Halfway between 50 and 60.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Park bar highlighted at value 55, between 50 and 60 lines.
Horizontal bar graph. Park bar highlighted. Multiple choice tool active.

Horizontal bar graph in creating mode. Scale of 10, axis 0-60. All bars empty. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

steps.text: Create the whole graph. Some values are at axis lines, some are between.
Complete the graph.

validator.description: Student set all four bars correctly

params.description: All bars now filled: Kai=35, Ava=50, Noah=25, Zoe=45.

steps.text: Ava at 50. Right at the line. Kai, Noah, and Zoe? All between the lines.

params.description: Data table hides from view.

steps.text: Use the graph you made. How many more miles did Ava walk than Noah?
How many more miles did Ava walk than Noah?
25 more miles. Ava walked 50, Noah walked 25. The difference is 25.
